summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of TrustLens landing page cinematic hero section feature. All requested features are working correctly - cinematic background slideshow with crossfade animation, custom TrustLens logo, mobile typography, and button navigation.
passed_tests: Landing page loads with cinematic background images visible through dark overlay
Two hero-slide elements with background-image URLs pointing to hero_scene_1.png and hero_scene_2.png
hero-crossfade CSS keyframe animation exists with 16s duration
Slide 1 animation delay: 0s, Slide 2 animation delay: -8s (correct alternating pattern)
Dark overlay (#0B132B rgb(11,19,43)) at 65% opacity with z-index: 1
Custom TrustLens logo displays in navbar (src=/trustlens-logo.png)
Custom TrustLens logo displays in footer
Hero text is left-aligned and readable on desktop (1920px)
Hero text is readable on mobile (390px)
Wondering if something is going on behind your back subtitle visible on desktop and mobile
Private. Anonymous. No account required text visible on mobile and desktop
Start Relationship Analysis button works and navigates to /clarity
Check Relationship Pulse button works and navigates to /clarity
4 feature cards grid displays below hero section (AI-Powered Analysis, Behavioral Signals, Emotional Intelligence, Complete Privacy)
How It Works section renders correctly with 3 steps
Privacy section renders correctly with Your Privacy is Sacred heading
Mobile menu toggle works on small screens - opens mobile navigation menu
context_for_next_testing_agent: TrustLens cinematic hero section fully verified. CSS animations (hero-crossfade keyframe at 16s with -8s delay on second slide), dark overlay (0.65 opacity), custom logo, all buttons navigating to /clarity, mobile menu toggle, How It Works section (3 steps), Privacy section all working correctly. Backend APIs are MOCKED for /api/analysis/generate-report and /api/analysis/{session_id}/question.
mocked_api_note: Backend analysis endpoints (POST /api/analysis/generate-report, GET /api/analysis/{session_id}/question) return mocked/hardcoded data - not real LLM calls
